div.h2: EPOLLOUT Busy Loop Anti-Pattern — Before vs After

g.text: AFTER: EPOLLOUT Registered Only When write_buf Non-Empty (reactor.c)
The EPOLLOUT Invariant (reactor.c — enforced in conn_arm_epollout / conn_disarm_epollout)
Diagnosis: How to Detect the Bug (shell)
EPOLLOUT registered at accept() — NEVER removed
fd=5 | EPOLLIN | EPOLLOUT | EPOLLET (armed on EAGAIN)
Useful work per call | ~8 requests dispatched
CPU core utilization | 4–18% (idle most of time)
CORRECT: CPU sleeps until real work exists
t=0.000ms | epoll_wait returns | EPOLLOUT | 0 bytes written
t=0.001ms | epoll_wait returns | EPOLLOUT | 0 bytes written
t=0.002ms | epoll_wait returns | EPOLLOUT | 0 bytes written
t=0.003ms | epoll_wait returns | EPOLLOUT | 0 bytes written
t=0.082ms | EPOLLIN on 8 fds | 8 requests read+processed
t=0.165ms | EPOLLIN on 12 fds | 12 requests processed
... | ~12,000 calls/sec | each call does real work
write_buf state | EPOLLOUT registered? | epoll_ctl call
write_buf empty (0 bytes) | NO | none (initial) or MOD to remove
write() returns EAGAIN | YES (arm now) | MOD EPOLLIN|EPOLLOUT
drain completes (empty) | NO (disarm now) | MOD EPOLLIN only
top / htop | one CPU core at 100%, no load spike
perf stat -p PID | very low IPC (< 0.3), high branch-misses
strace -p PID -e epoll_wait | epoll_wait returning 1 at ~1M/sec
ss -ti | rcv_buf=0 for all connections (no reads pending)
perf top | epoll_wait hot, no application symbols
Root cause: grep codebase for EPOLLOUT in EPOLL_CTL_ADD calls

text.tspan: /* ARM: only when buffer has data */
/* DISARM: immediately when buffer drains to 0 */
/* ARM: only when buffer has data */
/* DISARM: immediately when buffer drains to 0 */

g.text: send buf has space → fires again
